title: chamaecyparis thyoides f. andelyensis
intro: Chamaecyparis thyoides f. andelyensis, commonly known as the Atlantic white cedar, is a variety of evergreen tree native to the coastal regions of eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of the Atlantic white cedar include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
